person.name: Idowu David Awoyemi
person.affiliation: Department of Educational Leadership, Policy, and Technology Studies,
person.role: Instructional Technology Researcher | AI and XR in Education | Learning Designer

profile_summary: Doctoral candidate in Instructional Technology with a distinguished record of integrating teaching excellence, impactful research, and transformative service to advance educational innovation and equity. Recognized as the Most Outstanding Graduate Student in Research (2025) and recipient of the Alabama Power Innovation and Technology Award (2025), with expertise in immersive learning technologies, AI-enhanced education, and broadening participation in STEM fields.

education.institution: Federal University of Technology, Minna, Nigeria
Federal University of Technology, Minna, Nigeria

research_themes.title: Immersive Learning Systems
research_themes.body: Designs and studies virtual and mixed reality environments for authentic STEM learning, safety training, and performance transfer.

research_themes.title: AI-Enhanced Education
research_themes.body: Explores generative AI, AI literacy, instructional decision-making, and responsible integration of emerging tools in learning environments.

research_themes.title: Learning Analytics
research_themes.body: Uses multimodal evidence, behavioral traces, and statistical reasoning to understand how learners engage and improve over time.

research_themes.title: Equity and Participation
research_themes.body: Advances broadening participation in STEM and computing through inclusive design, teacher development, and responsive learning pathways.

featured_roles.title: Research Assistant
featured_roles.organization: The University of Alabama, Tuscaloosa, AL, United States
details: Developed an immersive virtual reality intervention for undergraduate engineering students, aiming to increase student engagement by 30% within one semester. Achieved a target of at least 80% of participating students reporting improved understanding of engineering concepts through surveys.
Conducted statistical analysis using advanced tools to derive meaningful insights from research data.
Orchestrated the systematic management of research data, ensuring accuracy and completeness.
Played a pivotal role in the development of manuscripts and research proposals.
Applied learner analytics techniques to assess and interpret data related to educational outcomes.
Demonstrated expertise in analyzing both qualitative and quantitative research data.
Co-designing, developing, and delivering professional development training for district teachers.
Co-designing, developing, and delivering computer-based physiological instruction to upper elementary students.

featured_roles.title: Teaching Assistant
featured_roles.organization: The University of Alabama, Tuscaloosa, AL, United States
details: Led discussion sections and facilitated active learning in instructional design courses, supporting over 15 students in mastering core concepts and applying theory to practice.
Designed and evaluated assessments, delivering timely and constructive feedback that enhanced student performance and engagement.
Held regular office hours and provided individualized academic support, contributing to positive student evaluations and improved learning outcomes.
Collaborated with faculty on curriculum development, incorporating inclusive teaching practices and innovative instructional materials.
Managed the Blackboard learning platform, monitored student progress, and communicated key updates to enrich the learning experience

design_entries.title: Instructional Design
design_entries.organization: The Office of Teaching Innovation and Digital Education, AL, United States
details: Proficiently utilized cutting-edge e-learning design tools to create engaging and interactive content. This involved hands-on experience with industry-standard tools such as Articulate Storyline 360, ensuring a seamless learning experience for users.
Effectively collaborated with Subject Matter Experts (SMEs) to extract relevant content and translate complex information into accessible and learner-friendly formats.
Developed a keen understanding of the importance of quality assurance matters in instructional design.
Demonstrated proficiency in navigating and utilizing Learning Management Systems, with a focus on creating a user-friendly interface and optimizing the learning experience for end-users.
Successfully integrated third-party tools to enhance the functionality and interactivity of e-learning content.
During my onboarding, I engaged in a well-designed course focused on optimizing new employee orientation.
Developed expertise in creating courses using Blackboard Learn, contributing to a seamless learning environment for students and educators.

details: Collaborated with faculty and staff to organize, design, and implement a week-long STEM camp for upper-grade elementary students.
Developed instructional materials and interactive lesson plans integrating coding, data science, and physiological computing concepts.
Designed and facilitated hands-on physiological activities (e.g., heart rate monitoring, data collection, and physical computing exercises) to promote experiential learning.
Assisted in coordinating daily instructional sessions, demonstrations, and research showcases, ensuring smooth program delivery.
Supported student engagement and learning outcomes through mentorship, guidance, and classroom facilitation.

research_case_studies.label: Immersive STEM Learning
research_case_studies.title: AI-Enhanced XR Learning for STEM Education: VR Hazard Identification in Civil Engineering
research_case_studies.subtitle: University of Alabama · Civil engineering safety training · Mixed-methods design and analytics
research_case_studies.problem: Construction safety instruction often depends on low-immersion demonstrations that make hazard recognition difficult to practice authentically before students enter high-risk settings.
stakeholders: Lab researchers designing VR learning tasks
Programs preparing students for safety-critical work
frameworks: Multimedia learning and immersive cognition principles
research_case_studies.solution: The project uses generative-AI-supported virtual environments and structured hazard-identification tasks to study how learners notice risks, shift strategies, and develop safer decision patterns in authentic contexts.
outcomes: Produced publishable work on immersive technology-enhanced learning and safety training.
Strengthened a research program connecting XR design with measurable performance evidence.
research_case_studies.reflection: This case anchors a broader research direction: immersive environments become more valuable when they are not only engaging, but also analytically transparent enough to reveal how expertise develops over time.

images.alt: VR hazard identification scenario with construction site
Night scaffold hazard markers in VR environment
AI avatar in immersive safety training environment

research_case_studies.label: AI Literacy Curriculum
research_case_studies.title: AI-WISE: Industry-Informed Artificial Intelligence Literacy Curriculum for Higher Education
research_case_studies.subtitle: University of Alabama · Undergraduate AI literacy design · Cross-disciplinary curriculum development
research_case_studies.problem: Students increasingly encounter AI tools in academic and professional settings, yet many programs still lack structured, ethical, and workforce-relevant AI literacy experiences.
frameworks: Backward design for outcome-aligned curriculum planning
Universal Design for Learning for multimodal access
Culturally responsive pedagogy for inclusive participation
research_case_studies.solution: AI-WISE organizes practical AI skills, ethical reasoning, and reflective evaluation into modular learning experiences that faculty can adapt without needing deep technical specialization.
outcomes: Created a reusable curriculum model for broader AI literacy integration.
Supported conference dissemination around practical and equitable AI education design.
research_case_studies.reflection: The strongest lesson here is that AI literacy is not only about tools. It is about helping learners interpret, question, and apply AI systems responsibly in disciplinary and civic contexts.

research_case_studies.label: Physiological Learning Analytics
research_case_studies.title: Beyond Self-Report: Using Eye-Tracking and Physiological Data to Evaluate Learning in Computing Environments
research_case_studies.subtitle: NSF ITEST context · Elementary computing experiences · Multimodal evaluation and broadening participation
research_case_studies.problem: Traditional evaluation methods often miss moment-to-moment evidence of attention, cognitive load, and engagement, especially with younger learners who may not fully articulate what they experienced.
stakeholders: Programs focused on equitable STEM pathways
frameworks: Learning analytics for multimodal evidence integration
research_case_studies.solution: This work integrates physiological and behavioral data with more familiar assessments to create a richer view of how learners experience computing tasks and where instructional support is needed.
outcomes: Contributed to NSF-supported research on computing participation and learner experience.
Extended evaluation practice beyond self-report toward more responsive evidence models.
research_case_studies.reflection: The project reinforced an important design belief: evaluation should not be an afterthought. It should be built into how we understand learning as it unfolds, especially in novel technology environments.
